name: Vegetarian Pot Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Start by making the crust. Sift flour, baking powder, and baking soda into a large bowl.
2. Using a pastry blender or fork, cut in margarine until it resembles coarse meal.
3. Combine buttermilk and honey; mix well with flour mixture.
4. Add more buttermilk if dough is too stiff. Mix thoroughly to form a stiff dough.
5. Knead dough on a lightly floured surface for 3 to 5 minutes until no longer sticky.
6. Roll out the dough into the desired shape.
7. Coat an 8-cup casserole dish with vegetable spray.
8. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
9. In a heavy saucepan over medium-high heat, heat wine to a simmer. Add onion and cook, stirring for 3 minutes.
10. Add celery, carrots, bell peppers, green beans, and peas. If mixture starts to dry out, add more wine. Cook, stirring about 3 minutes.
11. Reduce heat to low. Sprinkle flour over the vegetable mixture; cook, stirring for 2 minutes.
12. Combine skim milk and vegetable stock in a separate bowl.
13. Slowly whisk the liquid mixture into the vegetables. Sauce will start to thicken.
14. Add parsley, soy sauce, sage, thyme, pepper, and cayenne. Stir constantly until filling is thickened.
15. Remove from heat and pour into prepared casserole dish.
16. Lay biscuit crust lightly over the filling. Do not seal edges.
17. Bake 20 to 30 minutes or until crust is golden brown and filling is bubbling.
